summary: A layout field section the kind marks as an edge field is an edge slot — its entries are addresses, derived to ordinary edge rows by the same emit pass, a dangling entry refused

new.description: A layout member whose kind's edge fields name one of its field-section slots (`satisfies` among them) derives that section's entries into ordinary edge rows in the lock's existing declaration families; the edges join the same resolved enumeration the gate and read verbs share; a dangling entry hits the existing dangling-edge refusal, never a new one. Builds its kind facts by struct-update over `common::kind_facts` (tests/common/mod.rs:382) — never a new local KindFactRow literal.

edit.description: The layout reading surfaces an edge slot: a field section whose slot the kind's declared relationships (`relationships: Vec<Edge>`, the existing fact-5 edge fields) name reads as address entries, not only the verbatim span `LayoutReading::fields` holds today (Layout::read, kind.rs:245).

`derive_layout_rows` (drift.rs:601) widens: edge-slot entries lower into the same satisfies/edge declaration-row families the program's erased declarations land in — the emit merge point today extends the `nested_member` family and (since LAYOUT-PROSE-IMPORT) the `layout_import` family, never edges.

The derived edges join the one resolved enumeration gate, explain, and impact already range over (main.rs:1189 already folds mention and import edges into that set); a dangling satisfies entry resolves through the existing dangling-target refusal (contract.md, edge).

Existing layout-reading assertions the widened field-section reading may ripple into — update assertions/snapshots as needed. Same pass: `intent_kind_facts` (layout_kind.rs:146) hand-rolls the full KindFactRow literal — convert to struct-update over `common::kind_facts`, the pattern tests/lock_declaration_rows.rs already rides (engineering.md, 'One job, one home': test scaffolding is a surface too).
Existing lock row-family assertions the widened derive pass may ripple into — update assertions/snapshots as needed.

Consolidation only: the local `layout_kind` builder (layout_prose_import.rs:26) hand-rolls the full KindFactRow literal beside the one home — convert to struct-update over `common::kind_facts`; region/member helpers stay local (they duplicate nothing).

tests.asserts: an edge slot's entries derive to ordinary edge rows in the lock; the gate and read verbs see them in the one enumeration; a dangling entry refuses via the existing machinery

acceptance: A field section a layout kind's edge fields mark derives its entries into ordinary edge rows in the committed lock — satisfies among them — visible to the gate and read verbs; a dangling entry refuses through the existing refusal; no new declaration surface added; all gates green.
notes: Scoped at a0fccaf (0020: the derivation rides the existing kind edge-field fact over layout slots). No SDK schema delta — EdgeField (sdk/src/kind.ts:41) and LayoutRegionRow field slots exist. Unblocked at 7676c2b; anchors re-verified (derive_layout_rows drift.rs:601). Entry grain follows the frontmatter `satisfies` list-of-addresses semantics; prose spans stay prose (0020). Owns the layout kind-facts builder consolidation into tests/common (sweep 961c9c2).

summary: Composed prose declares an include — the target's contents pulled into the host's emitted bytes, fingerprinted in the lock, a declared edge, refusing loudly when dangling

new.description: An include declared in composed prose lands the target's contents byte-identical inside the host's emitted artifact; the dependency is fingerprinted in the lock so a moved target is drift; the edge joins the one resolved enumeration explain/impact narrate; a dangling include refuses before a byte is written (pipeline.md, Emit: 'Refusing' names the unresolvable include explicitly).

edit.description: The include declaration on composed prose — an intent apart from the mention: it moves content (pipeline.md, The SDK). Spelling is code's call (depth rule); the mention interpolation (the `MENTION_SLOT` sentinel in this file) is the precedent. The path resolves relative to the stating module, never the workspace.

A new include row family beside `mentionRows` (declarations.ts:299), carried across the seam in `Declarations` (mirror: `mentions`, src/drift.rs:1393) — a new `IncludeRow` ts-rs binding lands under sdk/src/generated/.

Engine-side resolve + fingerprint + pull-into-emitted-bytes. The resolution unifies with `resolve_layout_import` (drift.rs:655) — the consolidation LAYOUT-PROSE-IMPORT's notes sanctioned and d27a624 deferred for want of this second consumer; the fingerprint takes the same never-reaped source-dependency posture as `LayoutImportRow`.
The include edge reuses `ImportDeclaration`/`resolved_import_edges` (graph.rs:896/910) under the existing `IMPORT_FIELD` locus — path-resolved once at emit, no second lift.
The include edges fold into the same resolved reference set at the existing mention/import merge point (main.rs:1189).
Guards the generated seam bindings — the new `IncludeRow` binding ripples here; regenerate and re-assert.

tests.asserts: include content lands byte-identical in the emitted artifact; the dependency fingerprint reaches the lock; the edge is in the one enumeration; a dangling include refuses before any write

acceptance: A composed-prose include pulls its target's contents into the host's emitted bytes, fingerprinted in the committed lock and visible as a declared edge to the gate and read verbs; a dangling include refuses loudly before any byte is written; the resolver is one surface shared with the layout import; all gates green.
notes: Scoped at 961c9c2 (residue sweep): pipeline.md 'The SDK' has carried the include since e842a32 (0001/0002) with no entry ever operationalizing it; d27a624's body confirms no engine surface exists. The resolve_layout_import unification is the entry's named consolidation, not creep (engineering.md). Blocked behind LAYOUT-EDGE-SLOT: shared src/drift.rs, src/graph.rs.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, still at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at 7676c2b (ship audit): still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
